questions.question_text: Why did the king want no more talk about the hilsa-fish?
questions.question_html: Why did the king want no more talk about the hilsa-fish?

solutions.text: It was the season of Hilsa fish as everyone was busy talking about it. The king was fed off the talks about Hilsa-fish. More-over, he did not want his courtiers to waste their time on these talks which could affect their work.
solutions.html: It was the season of Hilsa fish as everyone was busy talking about it. The king was fed off the talks about Hilsa-fish. More-over, he did not want his courtiers to waste their time on these talks which could affect their work.

questions.question_text: What did the king ask Gopal to do to prove that he was clever?
questions.question_html: What did the king ask Gopal to do to prove that he was clever?

solutions.text: Time and again King used to test Gopal. So this time he asked Gopal to buy a hilsa-fish and bring it to the palace crossing the bazaar, without anyone asking anything about the fish throughout the way, to prove that he was clever.
solutions.html: Time and again King used to test Gopal. So this time he asked Gopal to buy a hilsa-fish and bring it to the palace crossing the bazaar, without anyone asking anything about the fish throughout the way, to prove that he was clever.

questions.question_text: What three things did Gopal do before he went to buy his hilsa-fish?
questions.question_html: What three things did Gopal do before he went to buy his hilsa-fish?

solutions.text: Gopal half-shaved his beard smeared himself with ash and wore disgraceful rags before he went to buy his hilsa-fish.
solutions.html: Gopal half-shaved his beard smeared himself with ash and wore disgraceful rags before he went to buy his hilsa-fish.

questions.question_text: How did Gopal get inside the palace to see the king after he had bought the fish?
questions.question_html: How did Gopal get inside the palace to see the king after he had bought the fish?

solutions.text: Gopal bought hilsa fish from the market and reached the king's palace in such getup. The guards refuse to open the gate for a mad man as they were unable to recognise him, They did not allow him to meet the king. Therefore, he began to dance and sing loudly. On hearing his loud song, the king sent his messenger to call him in the court.
solutions.html: Gopal bought hilsa fish from the market and reached the king's palace in such getup. The guards refuse to open the gate for a mad man as they were unable to recognise him, They did not allow him to meet the king. Therefore, he began to dance and sing loudly. On hearing his loud song, the king sent his messenger to call him in the court.

questions.question_text: Explain why no one seemed to be interested in talking about the hilsa-fish which Gopal had bought.
questions.question_html: Explain why no one seemed to be interested in talking about the hilsa-fish which Gopal had bought.

solutions.text: Gopal's funny appearance attracted the attention of people much more than the hilsa-fish. At that time, no one was caring about the hilsa-fish he was carrying. Everyone was busy in talking about his mad appearance, half shaved face and rags.
solutions.html: Gopal's funny appearance attracted the attention of people much more than the hilsa-fish. At that time, no one was caring about the hilsa-fish he was carrying. Everyone was busy in talking about his mad appearance, half shaved face and rags.

questions.question_text:
Write True or False against each of the following sentences:
The king lost his temper easily. ________
Gopal was a madman. ________
Gopal was a clever man. ________
Gopal was too poor to afford decent clothes. ________
The king got angry when he was shown to be wrong. ________
questions.question_html:
Write True or False against each of the following sentences:

  1. The king lost his temper easily. ________
  2. Gopal was a madman. ________
  3. Gopal was a clever man. ________
  4. Gopal was too poor to afford decent clothes. ________
  5. The king got angry when he was shown to be wrong. ________

questions.question_text:
Notice how in a comic book, there are no speech marks when characters talk. Instead what they say is put in a speech 'bubble'. However, if we wish to repeat or 'report' what they say, we must put it into reported speech.
Change the following sentences in the story to reported speech. The first one has been done for you.
How much did you pay for that Hilsa?
The woman asked the man how much he had paid for that Hilsa.
Why is your face half-shaven?
Gopal's wife asked him ________
I accept the challenge, Your Majesty.
Gopal told the king ________
I want to see the king.
Gopal told the guards ________
Bring the man to me at once.
The king ordered the guard ________
questions.question_html:
Notice how in a comic book, there are no speech marks when characters talk. Instead what they say is put in a speech ‘bubble’. However, if we wish to repeat or ‘report’ what they say, we must put it into reported speech.
Change the following sentences in the story to reported speech. The first one has been done for you.

  1. How much did you pay for that Hilsa?
    The woman asked the man how much he had paid for that Hilsa.
  2. Why is your face half-shaven?
    Gopal’s wife asked him ________
  3. I accept the challenge, Your Majesty.
    Gopal told the king ________
  4. I want to see the king.
    Gopal told the guards ________
  5. Bring the man to me at once.
    The king ordered the guard ________

solutions.text:
The woman asked the man how much did he pay for that Hilsa.
Gopal's wife asked him why his face was half-shaven.
Gopal told the king that he accepted the challenge.
Gopal told the guards that he wanted to see the king.
The king ordered the guard to bring the man to him at once.
solutions.html:
1. The woman asked the man how much did he pay for that Hilsa.
  2. Gopal's wife asked him why his face was half-shaven.
  3. Gopal told the king that he accepted the challenge.
  4. Gopal told the guards that he wanted to see the king.
  5. The king ordered the guard to bring the man to him at once.

questions.question_text:
Find out the meaning of the following words by looking them up in the dictionary. Then use them in sentences of your own.


challenge, mystic, comical, courtier, smearing
questions.question_html:
Find out the meaning of the following words by looking them up in the dictionary. Then use them in sentences of your own.

challenge, mystic, comical, courtier, smearing

solutions.text:
Challenge: A call to take part in a contest or competition.
Vikram accepted the challenge of his teacher and won a prize in the annual day celebrations.
Mystic: Spiritual, occult
The Sufi saints like Salim Chishti were mystic.
Comical: Funny
Mr Bean is one of the most famous comical characters on television.
Courtier: A person, who attends a royal court as a companion or advisor to the king or queen.
Birbal was one of the most important courtiers in Akbar's Court
Smearing: Coat or mark carelessly with grease or oil.
Before the game, Rakesh smeared his body with oil.
solutions.html:
Challenge: A call to take part in a contest or competition.
Vikram accepted the challenge of his teacher and won a prize in the annual day celebrations.
Mystic: Spiritual, occult
The Sufi saints like Salim Chishti were mystic.
Comical: Funny
Mr Bean is one of the most famous comical characters on television.
Courtier: A person, who attends a royal court as a companion or advisor to the king or queen.
Birbal was one of the most important courtiers in Akbar's Court
Smearing: Coat or mark carelessly with grease or oil.
Before the game, Rakesh smeared his body with oil.

questions.question_text: Look at the pictures and read the text aloud.
questions.question_html: Look at the pictures and read the text aloud.

questions.question_text:
Now ask your partner questions about each picture.
Where is the stag?
What is he doing?
Does he like his antlers (horns)?
Does he like his legs?
Why is the stag running?
Is he able to hide in the bushes?
Where are the hunters now?
Are they closing in on the stag?
Is the stag free?
What does the stag say about his horns and his legs?
questions.question_html:
Now ask your partner questions about each picture.

  1. Where is the stag?
  2. What is he doing?
  3. Does he like his antlers (horns)?
  4. Does he like his legs?
  5. Why is the stag running?
  6. Is he able to hide in the bushes?
  7. Where are the hunters now?
  8. Are they closing in on the stag?
  9. Is the stag free?
  10. What does the stag say about his horns and his legs?

solutions.text:
The stag is standing by the side of a pond.
The stag is about to drink water when he saw his reflection in the pond.
Yes, he finds them beautiful
No, the stag finds his legs to be thin and ugly.
The stag is running because he has been chased by the hunters.
No, he is not able to hide in the bushes because his horns got stuck in them.
The hunters are just behind the stag.
Yes, they are closing in on the stag.
The stag was able to run fast because of his legs and is free now.
The stag says that he was proud of his horns, but he would have been killed because of them. He was ashamed of his legs, but they saved his life.
solutions.html:
1. The stag is standing by the side of a pond.
  2. The stag is about to drink water when he saw his reflection in the pond.
  3. Yes, he finds them beautiful
  4. No, the stag finds his legs to be thin and ugly.
  5. The stag is running because he has been chased by the hunters.
  6. No, he is not able to hide in the bushes because his horns got stuck in them.
  7. The hunters are just behind the stag.
  8. Yes, they are closing in on the stag.
  9. The stag was able to run fast because of his legs and is free now.
  10. The stag says that he was proud of his horns, but he would have been killed because of them. He was ashamed of his legs, but they saved his life.

questions.question_text: Now write the story in your own words. Give it a title.
questions.question_html: Now write the story in your own words. Give it a title.

solutions.text:
The Stag and his Beautiful Horns
There was a stag, he lived in a jungle. One day he went to drink water in a pond. He saw his reflection in the pond. He felt proud of his beautiful antlers. Then he saw his legs, he was upset because they were thin and ugly. Suddenly, there were hunters behind him. The stag ran to save his life. He wanted to hide in the bushes, but couldn't as his antlers were stuck in them. He found the hunters just behind him. He ran for his life and was finally saved.
In the end, he realised that he was feeling proud of his antlers, but he would have been killed because of them. He felt ashamed of his legs but was able to save his life ultimately because of them.
solutions.html:
The Stag and his Beautiful Horns

There was a stag, he lived in a jungle. One day he went to drink water in a pond. He saw his reflection in the pond. He felt proud of his beautiful antlers. Then he saw his legs, he was upset because they were thin and ugly. Suddenly, there were hunters behind him. The stag ran to save his life. He wanted to hide in the bushes, but couldn't as his antlers were stuck in them. He found the hunters just behind him. He ran for his life and was finally saved.
In the end, he realised that he was feeling proud of his antlers, but he would have been killed because of them. He felt ashamed of his legs but was able to save his life ultimately because of them.

questions.question_text:
Complete the following word ladder with the help of the clues given below.
Clues:
Mother will be very ________ if you don’t go to school.
As soon as he caught ________ of the teacher, Mohan started writing.
How do you like my ________ kitchen garden? Big enough for you, is it?
My youngest sister is now a ________ old.
Standing on the _________, he saw children playing on the road.
Don’t make such a ________ Nothing will happen.
Don’t cross the _________ till the green light comes on.
questions.question_html:
Complete the following word ladder with the help of the clues given below.

Clues:

  1. Mother will be very ________ if you don’t go to school.
  2. As soon as he caught ________ of the teacher, Mohan started writing.
  3. How do you like my ________ kitchen garden? Big enough for you, is it?
  4. My youngest sister is now a ________ old.
  5. Standing on the _________, he saw children playing on the road.
  6. Don’t make such a ________ Nothing will happen.
  7. Don’t cross the _________ till the green light comes on.

solutions.text:
cross
sight
tiny
year
roof
fuss
street
